what_it_does: This file documents slides 35-49 of a ConceptV pitch-deck corpus, covering the closing grammar of a ~30-page long deck and a ~15-page condensed variant. It records per-slide attention flow, a large set of visual-design findings with recipes and generator rules, reusable components, and content-slot samples.
observations: Contains 15 detailed attention-flow entries, one per slide (p-35 through p-49).

Lists 40+ design findings grouped by category: gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, line-connector, texture, chrome-mode, zone, sequence, and attention-verbs.
Each finding includes a name, recipe, evidence slide(s), emergent flag, and generator rule.
Defines 11 reusable components such as ProductHeaderRow, TeamMemberCard, EqualPillarRow, TimelineBar, and HatchedSummaryBand.
Provides content_samples with slot names, character/word/line counts, and example text.
States a compression-invariance rule: the short deck omits slides rather than simplifying individual slides.
States an appendix-as-depth-layer grammar where the same full-detail slides serve both body and appendix roles.
Mandates a hatched summary band at the bottom of every informational slide as a 'slide contract'.
Uses a strict gold/amber-only accent palette with dark-grey body text and warm textured backgrounds.
Distinguishes two arrow types: outlined gold arrow for bullets and filled gold triangle for team-card separators.
standouts: Compression-invariance principle: a slide is an atomic unit; deck length changes by inclusion/omission, not by dumbing-down slides.
Appendix-as-depth-layer: the short deck points to an appendix that reuses the long deck's full-detail slides unchanged.
Hatched summary band is treated as a non-negotiable closing element on every content slide.
Full-bleed aspirational property photography is reserved for contact/closing slides, breaking the normal content grid.
Gold is the only accent color; all emphasis, borders, badges, and brand marks use the same warm amber family.
conventions: Follows a structured JSON schema with narrative_arc, attention_flow, findings, components, and content_samples.
Each finding uses a consistent recipe/evidence/generator_rule triplet.
Uses an emergent boolean to flag patterns that appear to arise from repeated use rather than being explicitly designed.
Components include a reuse_note linking them to specific slides.
Content samples include quantitative metrics (chars, words, lines) for each slot type.
Maintains neutral, descriptive language without ranking or prescribing preference.
concerns: Several generator rules are derived from a single slide (e.g., italic cover-only register, inverse CTA, TimingBadgeRow), which may overfit the corpus.
Some rules use absolute language ('every', 'always', 'never') based on only 15 observed slides.
No information about how pixel sizes, colors, or font names were measured or verified.
The 'emergent' flag criteria are not defined, so consistency across annotators is unclear.
Content slot examples mix directly observed text with inferred slot names, risking circular reuse guidance.
notes: A dense, rule-heavy design autopsy of the final third of a pitch deck, focused on turning visual patterns into reproducible generator instructions.
questions: How were color values, font sizes, and pixel dimensions measured from the source decks?
What is the relationship between this file and parts 1 and 2 of the same corpus?
What criteria determine whether a finding is marked emergent=true versus emergent=false?
Were the generator rules validated by attempting to regenerate the slides, or are they inferential?
Does the compression-invariance principle hold for other pitch decks beyond this corpus?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to add an interpretive layer for a ledger of code/design substrates, and the file does exactly that: it interprets a specific pitch-deck JSON extract into patterns, components, and rules.
apparent_themes: Precision and credibility in copy details
Reusability of components across deck variants
intention_signals: The file appears to be building a generative design system or style guide that can reproduce ConceptV pitch decks of varying lengths by reusing immutable slide atoms and applying a documented visual grammar.
novelty: The treatment of pitch-deck slides as immutable, reusable atoms with a formal compression-invariance rule, and the appendix as the same detail slides reused rather than separate simplified content.
contribution: A catalog of reusable visual components, generator rules, content-slot metrics, and structural principles (compression-invariance and appendix-as-depth) that a fused system could keep as a reproducible pitch-deck design grammar.
summary_for_embedding: A structured interpretive record of slides 35-49 of the ConceptV pitch-deck corpus, capturing attention flow, visual design findings, reusable components, content slot samples, and generator rules for both long and condensed deck variants.
intention_for_embedding: To extract and codify the closing visual grammar of a pitch deck so it can be reused, compressed, and extended as a generative design system.
